response.justification: Strong geospatial and distributed-systems domain expertise through GISCollective and Dunnhumby work, demonstrating the learning agility and systems thinking required. However, primary technical requirements are poorly matched: C++ experience is over a decade old, no Rust, and recent 5+ years focused on TypeScript/Node.js. Worth interviewing to assess current C++ capability and speed of ramp-up, but significant technical stack risk.
strengths: Deep geospatial domain knowledge as founder of GISCollective, contributor to Green Map, and FOSS4G speaker—directly relevant to routing/navigation work
Real distributed-systems experience handling millions of HTTP requests per second with async I/O, caching, and fiber-based concurrency at Dunnhumby
Demonstrated rapid learning across multiple languages and domains (DLang, Java, C#, TypeScript, C++) with strong open-source track record
concerns: Last C++ work was 2014–2015 at National Instruments (10+ years ago); no Rust experience despite being listed as acceptable alternative
Recent 5+ years entirely in TypeScript/Node.js and high-level systems; no evidence of modern systems-level language work or data-pipeline infrastructure at scale
No specific routing engine, tile optimization, or real-time traffic integration experience; GISCollective is a data-collection platform, not a routing system

key_factors.factor: C++ currency and modern systems programming
